Woof! Woof!
What has Biscuit found?
5

Is it a ball?
Woof!
6

Is it a bone?
Woof!
7

Quack!
It is a little duck.
8

The little duck is lost.
Woof! Woof!
9

We will bring
the little duck
back to the pond.
10

Woof! Woof!
11

Here, little duck.
Here is the pond.
12

Here are your mother
and your father.
Quack!
13

Here are your brothers
and your sisters.
Quack! Quack!
14

The ducks say thank you.
Thank
you for finding
the little duck.
15

Quack!
The little duck
wants to play.
16

Quack!
Woof!
17
Quack!
Woof!

Splash!
Biscuit fell into the pond!
18

Silly Biscuit.
You are all wet!
19

Woof!
Oh no, Biscuit.
Not a big shake!
20

Woof!
Time to go home, Biscuit.
21

Quack! Quack!
22

Say good-bye, Biscuit.
Woof! Woof!
Good-bye, little duck.
23

Biscuit has found
a new friend.

My
First
READING
When Biscuit finds a lost
duckling, he helps the
duckling find its way
home. That's when the fun
begins. Woof! Quack!
My Ideal for sharing with emergent readers
First
1 Simple sentences for eager new readers
2 High-interest stories for developing readers
3 Complex plots for confident readers
4 The perfect bridge to chapter books
